checks.name: host-node-timeout-still-reproduces
checks.detail: The isolated worktree still reproduces the original host-side Node HTTPS getMe timeout: raw/host-direct-node-getme.txt shows `fetch failed` with `UND_ERR_CONNECT_TIMEOUT` against api.telegram.org.

checks.name: bootstrap-install-fallback-regressions-covered
checks.detail: packages/bootstrap/src/install.test.ts and packages/bootstrap/src/install.runtime.test.ts now cover both the helper-level validated fallback path and the full install-path success case where a PowerShell-validated bot identity no longer degrades to warning state.

checks.name: bot-sendmessage-fallback-covered
checks.detail: apps/bot/src/index.ts now retries Windows sendMessage transport failures through PowerShell, and apps/bot/src/index.test.ts proves fallback success plus truthful handling of Telegram HTTP failures.

checks.name: live-worktree-token-validates-via-fallback
checks.detail: Current worktree artifacts raw/host-powershell-getme-worktree-current.txt, raw/host-bootstrap-getme-worktree-current.txt, and raw/install-json-worktree-current.txt show the updated isolated-worktree .env token validates @Gerta_homebot and allows the install path to pass the Telegram step via the PowerShell-assisted fallback.

checks.name: source-env-invalid-token-stays-truthful
checks.detail: The earlier source-worktree artifacts raw/host-powershell-getme-current.txt, raw/host-bootstrap-getme-current.txt, and raw/install-json.txt still show `401 Unauthorized`, proving invalid credentials are reported as configuration failure rather than being masked by the Windows transport fallback.

checks.name: repo-verification-passed
checks.detail: Full repo build, lint, typecheck, and test all passed in the isolated worktree; see raw/build.txt, raw/lint.txt, raw/typecheck.txt, and raw/test-integration.txt.
